title: Requests, Limits, QoS, Probes, Startup, Readiness, and Resource Failure · Cluster Case: K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests
summary: A slow-starting API is killed by liveness checks, serves traffic before warmup, and competes for memory without meaningful requests or limits. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s resource probe fault tests increment in health and resource behavior policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test cold start, overload, dependency loss, memory pressure, CPU throttling, termination, and recovery without turning probes into cascading failure.
Design HTTP, TCP, gRPC, or exec probes with correct target, timeout, period, thresholds, resource cost, failure budget, and diagnostic output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests · Theory K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory service passes liveness while serving stale data, has no startup probe, requests 100m but uses 750m CPU, and is OOMKilled under pressure. Redesign probes, requests, limits, QoS, graceful termination, and changed-load checks. Competency probe: Test cold start, overload, dependency loss, memory pressure, CPU throttling, termination, and recovery without turning probes into cascading failure.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service passes liveness while serving stale data, has no startup probe, requests 100m but uses 750m CPU, and is OOMKilled under pressure. Redesign probes, requests, limits, QoS, graceful termination, and changed-load checks. Competency probe: Test cold start, overload, dependency loss, memory pressure, CPU throttling, termination, and recovery without turning probes into cascading failure. A defensible solution applies K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to health and resource behavior policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-health-resources-qos-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-health-resources-qos-5
    artifact: health-resource-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s health resources qos 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      requests: {cpu: 200m, memory: 256Mi}
      limits: {memory: 512Mi}
      startupProbe: bounded-cold-start
      readinessProbe: traffic-contract
      livenessProbe: process-deadlock-only
      fault-test: throttling-and-oom
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl top pod status-api --containers
    failure: Reject dependency-based liveness, requests as runtime caps, green idle probes as capacity proof, or unexplained OOM and eviction.
    repair: Measure workload demand, separate probe purposes, calculate QoS, inject startup and pressure faults, and correlate container and node evidence.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A green readiness check under one idle run proves production capacity and resilience. This fails the K8s Resource Probe Fault Tests contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
